item.title: Trump dominated Davos. But Canada’s Carney was the star.
item.description: Carney delivered an address that squarely confronted the world wrought by Trump’s presidency. Davos’s attendees — including Trump — took notice.

item.title: Trump launches ‘Board of Peace’ at Davos, testing global order
item.description: Invitations to join the board have prompted a wide range of responses, from eager assent to hedging, wait-and-see statements from bewildered world leaders.

item.title: After ‘good’ Trump meeting, Zelensky pushes Europe hard to do more
item.description: President Trump and White House envoy Steve Witkoff have said a deal to resolve nearly four years of war between Russia and Ukraine is close.

item.title: Trump’s ‘master plan’ for Gaza contrasts with reality on the ground
item.description: Jared Kushner presented a vision for Gaza at the World Economic Forum in Davos that included high-rises, data centers and little input from Palestinians.

item.title: As Trump drops Greenland threats, Europeans say they are still on guard
item.description: European officials said they expect negotiations to touch on expanding the presence of U.S. troops and bases and greater access to investment in minerals.

item.title: European populists broke with Trump on Greenland as national goals diverged
item.description: The split between ideological allies showed the limits of the U.S. president’s with-me-or-against-me politics, and a key obstacle to cooperation among nationalist parties.

item.title: U.S. seeks to be ‘friends’ with Bangladesh’s once-banned Islamist party
item.description: In obtained audio recordings, a U.S. diplomat in Dhaka described how Washington wants to engage with Jamaat-e-Islami, potentially straining U.S-India ties.

item.title: Trump takes swipe at Canada after Carney’s Davos speech
item.description: Mark Carney said middle powers such as Canada could build a new order in an era of unfettered great powers, without directly mentioning the United States or its president.

item.title: U.S. military to move up to 7,000 ISIS detainees from Syria to Iraq
item.description: The mission followed a jailbreak earlier this week amid an advance by Syrian government forces into parts of the country long held by U.S. partners.

item.title: Israel kills 3 journalists in Gaza, including CBS News contributor
item.description: The journalists were killed Wednesday in a strike on their car in central Gaza. Israel’s military said it struck “several suspects” who were operating a drone.

item.title: Trump’s Greenland crusade pushes European allies to a breaking point
item.description: President Donald Trump’s ultimatum — give up Greenland or face a trade war — has convinced some European officials that retaliation, rather than conciliation, is the answer.

item.title: Elon Musk’s latest feud is a mudslinging match with a budget airline
item.description: The billionaire has been trading online barbs for days with Ryanair in a spat that started over the airline’s refusal to install Starlink. Ryanair has been leaning into it.

item.title: Immigration visas for people from these 75 countries are paused
item.description: The indefinite pause on issuing immigrant visas, announced by Trump last week, will turn away almost half of all legal immigrants over the next year, experts say.

item.title: Prince Harry takes the stand in privacy fight against Daily Mail publisher
item.description: During rare court testimony by a British royal, Harry rebutted suggestions that reporters obtained information legally by being part of his circle of friends.

item.title: Denmark fought alongside the U.S. Now, Trump is threatening it over Greenland.
item.description: Denmark was a committed partner to the U.S. during the war in Afghanistan, deploying thousands of troops, and made early contributions to the invasion of Iraq.

item.title: How Egypt is reviving the Suez Canal after maritime attacks sank traffic
item.description: Attacks by Houthi rebels in the Red Sea scared off many of the shipping firms that used the Suez Canal, depriving Egypt’s ailing economy of vital revenue.

item.title: In prudish China, men are detained after posts about ‘gay pandas’
item.description: The detentions come amid a broader crackdown on gay rights in recent years, with Chinese authorities shutting down gay dating apps and LGBTQ+ advocacy groups.
